title: Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County
text: Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County. Category: career_center. Type: workforce_development. Services: ["job_search", "resume_help", "WIOA_training_vouchers", "transportation_vouchers", "reentry_navigation"]. Eligibility: Open to all jobseekers; income-eligible for WIOA training vouchers. Address: 1200 Circle Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76119. Phone: 817-413-4400. Hours: Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. TWC anchor for Tarrant County. Always relevant for jobseekers. Reentry navigators on site.

title: Texas Workforce Commission - Online Portal
text: Texas Workforce Commission - Online Portal. Category: career_center. Type: state_workforce. Services: ["unemployment_claims", "job_search", "skills_training", "vocational_rehab"]. Eligibility: All Texas residents. Address: Statewide online services. Phone: 800-832-9394. Hours: 24/7 online; phone Mon-Fri 8am-6pm. Statewide TWC services. File unemployment, search jobs, find training programs.

title: Goodwill Industries Career Services
text: Goodwill Industries Career Services. Category: career_center. Type: training. Services: ["job_search", "resume_help", "computer_classes", "reentry_program", "career_training"]. Eligibility: Open to all jobseekers; reentry program for justice-involved. Address: 4005 Campus Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76119. Phone: 817-332-7866. Hours: Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. Free career classes, computer training, reentry support. Always relevant.

title: Fort Worth Public Library Career Resources
text: Fort Worth Public Library Career Resources. Category: career_center. Type: library. Services: ["resume_workshops", "computer_access", "online_job_search", "career_databases"]. Eligibility: Free to public. Address: 500 W 3rd St, Fort Worth, TX 76102. Phone: 817-392-7740. Hours: Mon-Thu 9am-9pm, Fri-Sat 9am-6pm, Sun 12-5pm. Free WiFi, computer terminals, career database access (LearningExpress, Brainfuse).

title: Trinity Metro Reduced Fare Program
text: Trinity Metro Reduced Fare Program. Category: social_service. Type: transportation. Services: ["transit_pass", "monthly_pass", "fare_assistance"]. Eligibility: Income-eligible (200% FPL), seniors 65+, persons with disabilities. Address: 1600 E Lancaster Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76102. Phone: 817-215-8600. Hours: Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. Reduced bus + TEXRail fare program. Apply with proof of income or disability.

title: Trinity Railway Express (TRE)
text: Trinity Railway Express (TRE). Category: social_service. Type: transportation. Services: ["commuter_rail", "DFW_to_Fort_Worth_service"]. Eligibility: Open to all riders. Address: 1001 Jones St, Fort Worth, TX 76102. Phone: 817-215-8600. Hours: Mon-Fri 5am-11pm; weekend service. Commuter rail Fort Worth to Dallas via 10 stations. Transit-friendly for DFW commute.

title: Catholic Charities Fort Worth - Car Repair Grants
text: Catholic Charities Fort Worth - Car Repair Grants. Category: social_service. Type: transportation. Services: ["car_repair_grants", "transportation_assistance", "case_management"]. Eligibility: Income-eligible Tarrant County residents. Address: 249 W Thornhill Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76115. Phone: 817-534-0814. Hours: Mon-Fri 9am-5pm. Up to $1500 for emergency car repairs needed for work. Application via case manager.

title: MITS - Mobility Impaired Transportation Service
text: MITS - Mobility Impaired Transportation Service. Category: social_service. Type: transportation. Services: ["paratransit", "door_to_door_transit"]. Eligibility: ADA-eligible riders unable to use fixed-route bus. Address: 1600 E Lancaster Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76102. Phone: 817-215-8600. Hours: Mon-Fri 5am-9pm; weekend service. ADA paratransit service for riders unable to use fixed-route bus. Application required.

title: TWC Child Care Services - Tarrant County
text: TWC Child Care Services - Tarrant County. Category: childcare. Type: subsidy. Services: ["childcare_subsidy", "Texas_Rising_Star_referral", "extended_hours_care"]. Eligibility: Working/training parents at or below 85% State Median Income. Address: 1200 Circle Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76119. Phone: 817-921-4636. Hours: Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. Income-based childcare subsidy. Pays directly to TRS-rated providers. 12-month enrollment.

title: Camp Fire First Texas
text: Camp Fire First Texas. Category: childcare. Type: early_childhood. Services: ["preschool", "after_school_program", "summer_camp", "infant_care"]. Eligibility: Open to all; subsidies accepted. Address: 2700 Meacham Blvd, Fort Worth, TX 76137. Phone: 817-831-2111. Hours: Mon-Fri 6:30am-6:30pm. Texas Rising Star 4-star center. Accepts TWC subsidies. Multiple Tarrant locations.

title: Action Plan: No Daytime Childcare
text: Connect client with Texas Workforce Commission Child Care Services (CCS) subsidy and local Head Start centers. Verify eligibility for CCS through Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County and assist with application.

title: Action Plan: No Evening Childcare
text: Identify licensed evening care providers in the Tarrant County area. Explore family support networks and employer-sponsored backup care options.

title: Action Plan: No Infant Care Available
text: Check waitlists at licensed infant care centers. Connect with Early Head Start of Tarrant County and home-based care provider networks for immediate openings.

title: Action Plan: Unaware of Childcare Subsidies
text: Educate client on Texas Workforce Commission Child Care Services (CCS) subsidy eligibility. Walk through application process and provide contact info for Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County.

title: Action Plan: Childcare Unaffordable
text: Calculate childcare cost vs. income ratio. Apply for sliding-scale fee programs and explore employer childcare benefits or co-op arrangements.

title: Action Plan: No Personal Vehicle
text: Map Trinity Metro routes from client's home to potential employers. Connect with vehicle assistance programs and explore carpool matching services.

title: Action Plan: No Public Transit Access
text: Identify alternative transportation: Trinity Metro ZIPZONE on-demand service, vanpools, employer shuttles. Consider relocation assistance if housing is flexible.

title: Action Plan: No Night Shift Transportation
text: Check employer willingness to adjust shifts. Explore ride-share stipends, employer shuttle programs, and bicycle commuting for short distances.

title: Action Plan: Unreliable Transportation
text: Connect with auto repair assistance programs. Establish backup transportation plan with Trinity Metro, carpool, or ride-share as contingency.

title: Action Plan: Transportation Cost Too High
text: Calculate commute cost vs. income. Apply for Trinity Metro reduced-fare program, explore employer transit benefits, and consider closer employment options.

title: Action Plan: Low Credit Score
text: Pull free credit report to identify specific issues. Create a credit improvement plan targeting highest-impact items first. Connect with credit counseling.

title: Action Plan: No Credit History
text: Open a secured credit card or credit-builder loan. Enroll in rent reporting programs to build credit history from existing payments.

title: Action Plan: High Debt Burden
text: Create debt inventory and prioritize by interest rate. Explore debt consolidation, income-driven repayment plans, and negotiate with creditors.

title: Action Plan: Accounts in Collections
text: Verify debt validity under FDCPA. Negotiate pay-for-delete agreements where possible. Connect with Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas for disputed or invalid debts.

title: Action Plan: No Bank Account
text: Identify second-chance banking programs at local credit unions. Assist with opening a basic checking account to enable direct deposit.

title: Action Plan: Unstable Housing
text: Connect with emergency housing programs and shelters. Apply for rapid rehousing assistance and establish stable address for employment applications.

title: Action Plan: Housing Unaffordable
text: Apply for Section 8 or public housing waitlists with Fort Worth Housing Solutions. Explore shared housing options and negotiate with landlord for reduced rent or payment plan.

title: Action Plan: Eviction on Record
text: Connect with Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas to review eviction record for sealing or expungement eligibility. Identify landlords who accept applicants with eviction history.

title: Action Plan: No Health Insurance
text: Screen for Medicaid/CHIP eligibility and assist with enrollment. Check marketplace plans during open enrollment. Connect with JPS Health Network sliding-scale clinics for immediate care.

title: Action Plan: Physical Disability
text: Document accommodation needs under ADA. Connect with Texas Workforce Solutions Vocational Rehabilitation for job placement support and workplace modification assistance.

title: Action Plan: Mental Health Challenges
text: Connect with mental health providers accepting Medicaid or sliding-scale fees (JPS Behavioral Health, MHMR of Tarrant County). Explore Employee Assistance Programs and workplace accommodation options.

title: Action Plan: Substance Use Disorder
text: Connect with SAMHSA treatment locator and local recovery programs. Support treatment-compatible work schedules and sober living arrangements.

title: Action Plan: No Professional Certification
text: Identify relevant certifications for target industry. Connect with WIOA-funded training programs (Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County) and scholarship opportunities for certification exams.

title: Action Plan: Skills Gap
text: Conduct skills assessment to identify specific gaps. Enroll in targeted short-term training at Tarrant County College or workforce development center.

title: Action Plan: No GED or High School Diploma
text: Enroll in GED preparation program at Tarrant County College or local adult education center. Explore employer-sponsored GED programs and online preparation resources.

title: Action Plan: Outdated Job Skills
text: Assess transferable skills and identify in-demand fields. Connect with retraining programs and apprenticeship opportunities in growing industries.

title: Action Plan: Criminal Record
text: Research ban-the-box and fair-chance employers in the Tarrant County area. Explore order of nondisclosure and expunction eligibility through Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas, and connect with reentry employment programs.

title: Action Plan: Background Check Barrier
text: Identify fair-chance employers who evaluate candidates individually. Prepare disclosure statement and gather character references for applications.

title: Action Plan: Reentry from Incarceration
text: Connect with reentry programs (Cornerstone Assistance Network, Catholic Charities Fort Worth) for job readiness training. Assist with ID documents, resume development, and transitional employment placement.

title: Action Plan: Extended Employment Gap
text: Develop narrative explaining gap positively. Fill with volunteer work, training, or freelance experience. Practice interview responses for gap questions.

title: Action Plan: Low Income / Underemployment
text: Identify higher-paying opportunities matching client skills. Connect with career advancement programs and negotiate for raises or promotions at current employer.

title: Action Plan: Limited Work Hours Available
text: Address root constraints first (childcare, transportation). Explore remote work options and employers offering flexible scheduling.

title: Action Plan: Job Instability
text: Identify and address root-cause barriers creating instability. Build emergency fund plan and develop retention strategies with employer support.
